mnemonics: ND-Array: N-Dimensional Data, Dazzlingly Arranged for Rapid Analysis
keyPoints: Ndarray is the core data structure of NumPy, representing a multi-dimensional, homogeneous array of fixed-size items.
It supports vectorized operations, allowing for efficient mathematical computations on entire arrays without explicit loops.
Arrays have attributes like shape (dimensions), dtype (data type), and size (total number of elements), which define their structure and capabilities.
commonMistakes: Attempting to perform operations on arrays of incompatible shapes (e.g., (3,) and (3,1)), leading to broadcasting errors or unexpected results.
Confusing Python lists with NumPy arrays and trying to use list methods (like .append()) on an ndarray, which does not work.
Using the wrong data type (dtype) for an array, which can lead to inefficient memory usage or unintended mathematical results (e.g., integer division instead of float).
